summary:
Acidophilic owl-eyed nuclei seen under microscopy along with positive staining for both CD15 and CD30 are highly indicative of nodular sclerosing type of HL; thus,
The diagnosis suggested here is \(\textbf{nодулярно-склеротическая форма холемы Ходжкина}\) or Nodular Sclerosis Hodgkin's Lymphoma based on these characteristic features observed clinically. Agent2 will now provide its summary considering only critical aspects without additional details:

In a 32-year-old woman where biopsied left supraclavicular lymph nodes show acidophilic owl-eye nuclei stained positively by CD15 and CD30, the most likely diagnosis is
Nodular Sclerosis Type of Hodgkin Lymphoma due to distinctive morphological and immunophenotypic characteristics. Thus,\( \textbf{\underline{Option\:A}}\) fits best as per provided evidence. 

This concise version captures essential elements while adhering strictly within two-sentence limits focusing directly upon core diagnostic indicators mentioned initially.
